title: Fix Services page card layout alignment and remove inconsistent gaps

requirements.summary: Fix service card grid layout so all 15 cards align to the top of their grid cells with dynamic height based on content
acceptanceCriteria: All 15 service cards start from the same top edge within their grid row.
No card has a fixed height or min-height CSS property applied.
Cards with fewer sub-service items are not stretched to match taller neighbors.
Cards with more sub-service items expand naturally without overflow or clipping.

file_operations.description: Apply `items-start` (align-items: start) to the grid container element that wraps all service cards. Remove any `h-full`, `min-h-*`, or fixed height classes from individual service card elements. Ensure each card's height is determined by its content only.

requirements.summary: Replace the card grid container with a clean responsive CSS grid with equal columns and consistent gaps
acceptanceCriteria: All columns have equal width at each breakpoint.
The gap between all adjacent cards (both horizontal and vertical) is identical.
No individual card has extra margin-top, margin-bottom, or padding overrides that differ from siblings.
Grid renders as 1 column on mobile (< 640px), 2 columns on tablet (640px–1023px), and 3 columns on desktop (≥ 1024px).

file_operations.description: Replace the existing grid container classes with `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6`. Remove any irregular margin or padding classes (e.g., `mt-*`, `mb-*`, `mx-*`, `my-*`) from individual service card elements to ensure uniform spacing across all cards.

requirements.summary: Apply uniform internal padding to every service card with identical padding on all four sides
acceptanceCriteria: Every service card uses the same padding value on all four sides.
No individual card has a different padding class or inline style overriding the shared padding.
Card content does not touch the card border on any side.

file_operations.description: Apply a consistent Tailwind padding class (e.g., `p-6`) to all service card elements. Remove any per-card padding overrides (e.g., `px-*`, `py-*`, `pt-*`, `pb-*`, `pl-*`, `pr-*`) that differ between cards. Ensure every card has the same padding class applied identically.

requirements.summary: Enforce uniform vertical spacing between internal card elements (icon → title → description → sub-service list) across all 15 cards
acceptanceCriteria: The gap between the icon and the card title is identical on all 15 cards.
The gap between the title and the description is identical on all 15 cards.
The gap between the description and the sub-service list is identical on all 15 cards.
Sub-service list items have consistent spacing between them on all cards.

file_operations.description: Apply consistent Tailwind spacing utilities (e.g., `space-y-3` or individual `mb-*` classes) to the internal structure of every service card. Ensure the gap between icon, title, description, and sub-service list is identical across all 15 cards. For the sub-service list, apply consistent spacing classes (e.g., `space-y-2`) to list items.

requirements.summary: Verify responsive layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile with correct alignment and no overflow while maintaining existing branding
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop (≥ 1024px): 3-column grid with all cards top-aligned and evenly spaced.
On tablet (640px–1023px): 2-column grid with all cards top-aligned and evenly spaced.
On mobile (< 640px): 1-column stack with uniform card padding and no horizontal overflow.
All existing card colors, icons, typography, border radius, and shadow styles are unchanged.
No card content is truncated or hidden due to the layout changes.

file_operations.description: Verify the responsive grid behavior by testing the layout at desktop (≥ 1024px), tablet (640px–1023px), and mobile (< 640px) breakpoints. Ensure no card overflows or misaligns. Confirm all existing dark navy (#0a1628) background, golden yellow (#f5c518) accent colors, card border/shadow styles, icons, and typography remain unchanged. Only layout and spacing classes should be modified.
